content: For wheat, optimal soil pH is between 6.0 and 7.0. It requires moderate rainfall (30-90cm). Excessive nitrogen fertilizer can cause lodging, leading to crop loss.
Rice requires heavy irrigation, often thriving in standing water. The ideal pH is 5.5 to 6.5. High temperatures (20-35°C) and high humidity are necessary for optimal growth.
Soil pH management is critical. To increase pH (reduce acidity), apply agricultural lime. To lower pH (increase acidity), add elemental sulfur or aluminum sulfate.

Fertilizer optimization: Apply nitrogen in split doses rather than all at once to prevent leaching and improve uptake efficiency. Use precision farming tools to assess local soil nutrient deficiencies.
Corn yields peak with well-drained loamy soils and warm weather. It is highly responsive to nitrogen but susceptible to drought stress during the silking stage.
